description: Multi-agent reliability scenarios for PolyPilot. Tests cover every orchestration lifecycle path: group creation, dispatch, worker execution, synthesis, subsequent orchestrations, follow-up handling, restart recovery, and state hygiene. Complements multi-agent-scenarios.json which covers organization/presets/squad. Execute against a running PolyPilot instance using MauiDevFlow CDP commands.

prerequisites.notes: All test sessions MUST point to the PolyPilot repository. Use 'Implement & Challenge' preset (ignore PR Reviewer). Never commit, push, or create GitHub issues/PRs. DevFlow agent port is 9223. CDP Blazor clicks require PointerEvent dispatch.

scenarios.name: Create Implement & Challenge multi-agent group
scenarios.description: Create a multi-agent group using the Implement & Challenge preset. Verify orchestrator and workers are created with correct roles.
invariants: All sessions point to PolyPilot repo

scenarios.name: Multi-agent group shows all sessions in sidebar
scenarios.description: Verify all group sessions (orchestrator + workers) are visible in the sidebar under the group.
invariants: All sessions visible under group header
Session count matches expected (orchestrator + N workers)

scenarios.name: Workers have model assignments from preset
scenarios.description: Verify each worker session has its configured model from the preset.
invariants: Each worker has a non-empty model assignment

steps.text: Verify by expanding each worker card and checking model dropdown, or via event log on first dispatch

scenarios.name: Orchestrator session has correct metadata
scenarios.description: Verify the orchestrator session is marked with the correct role and group membership.
invariants: Session is part of the multi-agent group

steps.note: Will be verified when first message is sent

scenarios.name: Orchestration dispatches to all workers
scenarios.description: Send a prompt to the group. Verify the orchestrator dispatches tasks to all workers via event log.
invariants: Event log shows [DISPATCH-ROUTE] with correct mode
Event log shows [SEND] for each worker
Event log shows [DISPATCH] with worker names

steps.text: Add a one-line code comment to the top of README.md explaining what PolyPilot is. Keep it simple.

steps.note: Wait for orchestrator to plan and dispatch

scenarios.name: Workers dispatched with stagger delay
scenarios.description: Verify workers are dispatched approximately 1 second apart, not all at once.

scenarios.name: PendingOrchestration file created during dispatch
scenarios.description: During worker execution, verify pending-orchestration.json exists with correct data.
invariants: Contains correct group ID and worker names

scenarios.name: Orchestrator planning includes @worker blocks
scenarios.description: Verify the orchestrator's response contains @worker task assignments.
invariants: Event log shows 'Early dispatch: @worker blocks detected'

scenarios.name: Workers process their tasks independently
scenarios.description: Monitor workers during execution. Each should cycle through TurnStart/TurnEnd events independently.
invariants: Each worker has AssistantTurnStartEvent and AssistantTurnEndEvent in event log

steps.note: Wait for workers to start processing

scenarios.name: Workers using tools complete without getting stuck
scenarios.description: Workers often use tools (grep, file reads). Verify tool execution completes.
invariants: No workers stuck with HasUsedToolsThisTurn=true indefinitely

steps.note: Wait for at least one worker to complete

scenarios.name: Worker IDLE-DEFER fires and resolves for subagents
scenarios.description: If a worker dispatches sub-agents (task tool), IDLE-DEFER should fire and eventually resolve.
invariants: If [IDLE-DEFER] appears, it's eventually followed by [COMPLETE]
Worker doesn't stay stuck in IDLE-DEFER forever

steps.text: This scenario is best verified by checking event log after orchestration completes

scenarios.name: Worker timeout produces error result, doesn't block orchestration
scenarios.description: If a worker times out (>10 min), the orchestration should still continue with an error result.
invariants: Orchestration doesn't hang forever waiting for a dead worker
Synthesis includes failure message for timed-out worker

steps.text: Difficult to force a timeout in live testing. Verify by checking event log for any [WATCHDOG] entries on workers and confirming orchestration still completed.

scenarios.name: One worker error doesn't block the whole orchestration
scenarios.description: If one worker fails, the other worker should still complete and synthesis should include the failure.
invariants: Orchestration completes even if one worker errored
Synthesis prompt includes error message for failed worker

steps.text: Verify from event log: if any [ERROR] entries exist for workers, check that [DISPATCH] Collected results still fires

scenarios.name: Synthesis includes all worker responses
scenarios.description: After workers complete, verify the synthesis prompt is sent to the orchestrator with all results.
invariants: Event log shows 'Collected N/N worker results for synthesis'

scenarios.name: Orchestrator completes after synthesis
scenarios.description: After synthesis prompt is sent, the orchestrator should process it and complete.

scenarios.name: PendingOrchestration file cleared after synthesis
scenarios.description: After orchestration completes, pending-orchestration.json should be empty or deleted.
invariants: File is empty, contains '{}', or doesn't exist

scenarios.name: All workers have IsProcessing=false after orchestration
scenarios.description: After orchestration completes, verify every worker session is idle.
invariants: Every worker session has IsProcessing = false
No stop buttons visible for any worker

scenarios.name: No orphaned worker state after orchestration
scenarios.description: After orchestration, no workers should be stuck in a partial processing state.

steps.command: for w in 1 2; do sends=$(grep -c "\[SEND\].*worker-$w" ~/.polypilot/event-diagnostics.log); completes=$(grep -c "\[COMPLETE\].*worker-$w" ~/.polypilot/event-diagnostics.log); echo "worker-$w: sends=$sends completes=$completes"; done

scenarios.name: Second orchestration to same group completes
scenarios.description: After one orchestration completes, send a new prompt to the same group. Verify full lifecycle works again.

steps.text: Now add a comment to the bottom of README.md saying 'Built with GitHub Copilot'. Keep it simple.

steps.note: Wait for second orchestration to complete

scenarios.name: Third orchestration shows no accumulated state issues
scenarios.description: A third consecutive orchestration should work as cleanly as the first.
invariants: No accumulated stuck state from previous orchestrations
Event log clean for this orchestration

steps.text: Check if README.md has any typos and fix them. If none, just say so.

scenarios.name: Orchestration works after a previous worker error
scenarios.description: If a previous orchestration had a worker error, the next one should still work.
invariants: No lingering error state from previous run

steps.text: This is verified by the second/third orchestration tests above. If any had errors, this confirms recovery.

scenarios.name: Follow-up message during orchestration is queued, not steered
scenarios.description: Send a follow-up while workers are busy. It should be queued (not steer the orchestrator). PR #375 fix.
invariants: Event log shows QUEUED, not STEER
Follow-up processed after current orchestration completes

steps.text: Review all the files in PolyPilot/Models/ directory.

scenarios.name: Follow-up after orchestration completes starts new orchestration
scenarios.description: Send a message after orchestration is fully complete. It should start a new orchestration.

steps.text: Summarize what changes were made across all previous tasks.

scenarios.name: Aborting orchestration cleans up all sessions
scenarios.description: Abort the orchestrator while workers are running. Verify clean state for all sessions.

steps.text: Do a comprehensive review of every single file in the repository.

scenarios.name: Multi-agent group persists across app relaunch
scenarios.description: Relaunch the app and verify the multi-agent group is restored with all sessions.
invariants: Group appears in sidebar after relaunch

scenarios.name: No phantom or duplicate worker sessions after relaunch
scenarios.description: After relaunch, verify no duplicate worker sessions or phantom '(resumed)' entries.

scenarios.name: PendingOrchestration recovers after relaunch during dispatch
scenarios.description: If the app relaunches while workers are processing, PendingOrchestration should enable recovery.
invariants: If pending-orchestration.json existed, recovery was attempted
Workers either completed or were collected with partial results

steps.text: Best tested by: 1) Start orchestration, 2) Immediately relaunch, 3) Check event log for 'Resuming pending orchestration'

steps.command: grep 'Resuming pending orchestration\|pending orchestration' ~/.polypilot/event-diagnostics.log | tail -3 || echo 'no pending recovery'

scenarios.name: New orchestration works after relaunch
scenarios.description: After relaunching and restoring the group, send a new prompt. Verify full lifecycle works.

steps.text: What is in the README.md file? Summarize it briefly.

scenarios.name: No workers stuck in IsProcessing after relaunch
scenarios.description: After relaunch, verify no worker sessions are stuck processing.
invariants: All workers have IsProcessing = false within 30s of relaunch

scenarios.name: Every [DISPATCH] has completion in event log
scenarios.description: Audit the event log: every worker dispatch should have a completion.

scenarios.name: No unresolved TaskCompletionSources after orchestration
scenarios.description: After orchestration, verify no TCS objects are waiting for completion.

steps.text: TCS state is internal — best verified by confirming orchestrator can accept new messages

scenarios.name: Watchdog doesn't kill workers that are actively processing
scenarios.description: During a long orchestration, verify the watchdog doesn't prematurely kill active workers.
invariants: No [WATCHDOG].*kill entries for workers with recent events
Watchdog Case B defers correctly when events.jsonl is fresh

scenarios.name: IDLE-DEFER fires and resolves without leaving sessions stuck
scenarios.description: If IDLE-DEFER fires during worker execution, it should eventually resolve to completion.
invariants: Every [IDLE-DEFER] is eventually followed by [COMPLETE] for the same session
No sessions left in permanent IDLE-DEFER state

scenarios.name: Reconnect during orchestration doesn't cause deadlock
scenarios.description: If a reconnect happens mid-orchestration, TCS should be cancelled and orchestration should not deadlock.
invariants: Orchestration either recovers or fails cleanly

steps.text: Best tested by triggering a reconnect mid-orchestration (e.g., server restart). In live testing, verify no sessions are stuck after any reconnect events in the log.

scenarios.name: Deleting multi-agent group removes all sessions cleanly
scenarios.description: Delete the test multi-agent group. Verify all sessions are removed with no orphans.
invariants: All group sessions removed from sidebar
